2606.27881.title: A Study of Temporal Fusion Strategies for Named Entity Recognition in Historical Texts
2606.27881.paper_summary: Temporal variation poses a unique challenge for named entity recognition (NER) in historical texts, where entities drift in surface form and salience across time. While language models (LMs) have made progress in various NLP tasks, their ability to reason about temporality, especially in diachronic contexts, remains limited or at least, questionable. In this paper, we systematically study how temporal metadata can be structurally embedded into NER models using a range of lightweight fusion strategies. We experiment with both absolute and relative temporal representations, injected into Transformer-based architectures via early or late fusion mechanisms such as cross-attention, adapters, and concatenation. Our evaluations on French and German historical datasets reveal that late fusion strategies yield more robust and temporally generalisable performance, particularly in early and noisy periods.
2606.27881.paper_summary_zh: 時間變化對於歷史文本中的命名實體識別 (NER) 提出了獨特的挑戰，因為實體在表面形式和顯著性上隨著時間而漂移。雖然語言模型 (LMs) 在各種自然語言處理任務中取得了進展，但它們對於時間性的推理能力，特別是在歷時語境中，仍然有限或至少是值得懷疑的。在本文中，我們系統地研究了如何將時間元數據結構性地嵌入到 NER 模型中，使用一系列輕量級的融合策略。我們實驗了絕對和相對的時間表示，通過早期或晚期融合機制（如交叉注意力、適配器和串接）注入到基於 Transformer 的架構中。我們對法語和德語歷史數據集的評估顯示，晚期融合策略產生了更穩健且時間上可泛化的性能，特別是在早期和噪音較多的時期。
2606.27881.author: Emanuela Boros et.al.
